title: [InstCombine] Replace alloca with undef size with poison instead of null
author: mgcarrasco
description:
InstCombine previously replaced an alloca instruction with a null pointer when the array size operand was undef. While this replacement may be legal, it still caused invalid IR in cases where the original alloca was used by `@llvm.lifetime` intrinsics.

The spec requires that the pointer operand of `@llvm.lifetime.*` must be either:

- a pointer to an alloca instruction, or
- a poison value.

Replacing the pointer with null violated this requirement and triggered verifier errors.

These new changes update InstCombine so that in this scenario the alloca is replaced with poison instead of null.

tests.test_body:
; InstCombineLoadStoreAlloca replaced alloca uses with null when the
; alloca size was poison. This causes invalid IR:
;
;   call void @llvm.lifetime.start(ptr %i_)
;   becomes:
;   call void @llvm.lifetime.start(ptr null)
;
; According to the lifetime intrinsic spec, the argument must be either:
;   - A pointer to an alloca instruction, or
;   - A poison value.
;
; Since null is neither an alloca pointer nor poison, this transformation
; produced invalid IR.

define i32 @foo() {
;
entry:
  %i_ = alloca i32, i32 poison, align 4
  call void @llvm.lifetime.start(ptr %i_)
  store i32 0, ptr %i_, align 4
  ret i32 0
}

comments.author: github-actions[bot]
comments.body:
<!--LLVM CODE FORMAT COMMENT: {undef deprecator}-->

:white_check_mark: With the latest revision this PR passed the undef deprecator.

comments.author: nikic
comments.body:
> We need to actually fix this. If we end up replacing an alloca with poison we should go through its users and remove any lifetime annotations.

That's not necessary, we explicitly allow poison lifetime markers to support common poison rauw patterns. The problem here is just that the alloca is being replaced with null. We should replace it with poison instead.

Or maybe better, with non-terminal unreachable + poison. llubi treats alloca with poison size as UB, which makes sense to me: https://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/blob/03b70cb1f45cc4ffb0ffbd0a832ff86e93833de0/llvm/tools/llubi/lib/Interpreter.cpp#L806-L807 It looks like LangRef currently doesn't spell this case out, but that seems like the correct behavior to spec.

comments.author: boomanaiden154
comments.body:
> That's not necessary, we explicitly allow poison lifetime markers to support common poison rauw patterns. The problem here is just that the alloca is being replaced with null. We should replace it with poison instead.

Ah, didn't realize that the assertion also accepted `poison`. Replacing the alloca with `poison` makes sense to me.

> It looks like LangRef currently doesn't spell this case out, but that seems like the correct behavior to spec.

Probably not a terrible idea to spell it out? I can put up a patch.

comments.author: jmmartinez
comments.body:
> The problem here is just that the alloca is being replaced with null. We should replace it with poison instead.

Just asking, is it legal to replace `alloca i32 undef` with `ptr null` in the first place? I'd expect an arbitrary valid value among `alloca i32 0...N`, but `null` sounds odd to me.

comments.author: nikic
comments.body:
> > It looks like LangRef currently doesn't spell this case out, but that seems like the correct behavior to spec.
> 
> Probably not a terrible idea to spell it out? I can put up a patch.

That would be good, thanks!

> > The problem here is just that the alloca is being replaced with null. We should replace it with poison instead.
> 
> Just asking, is it legal to replace `alloca i32 undef` with `ptr null` in the first place? I'd expect an arbitrary valid value among `alloca i32 0...N`, but `null` sounds odd to me.

Assuming that `alloca i32 undef` is UB, any value is valid.

comments.author: mgcarrasco
comments.body:
Thanks for the feedback.

I pushed a fix. Now the alloca is replaced with poision instead of null, fixing the reported test.

Proof: https://alive2.llvm.org/ce/z/USvVCd

The code formatter is reporting a false positive. It greps the "undef" in the comment.

-----

Proof (only instcombine): https://alive2.llvm.org/ce/z/3c4KLK

comments.author: mgcarrasco
comments.body:
> PR title/description need to be updated to match what the patch actually does now.

Thanks. Also slightly edited the commit message.

comments.body:
> > PR title/description need to be updated to match what the patch actually does now.
> 
> Thanks. Also slightly edited the commit message.

The commit message will be taken from the pull request description, not the commit. So the PR description should not include something like "Original PR description:"

comments.author: mgcarrasco
comments.body: To avoid wreaking havoc, do you know if it is safe merging the changes despite the formatting error? This is because "undef" is grepped in the test, and it matches with the comments (false positive).

comments.author: nikic
comments.body:
> To avoid wreaking havoc, do you know if it is safe merging the changes despite the formatting error? This is because "undef" is grepped in the test, and it matches with the comments (false positive).

Yes, it's fine. Though for the comment, it's really unnecessary to talk about undef at all, esp. as the test case actually uses poison.

comments.author: boomanaiden154
comments.body:
> To avoid wreaking havoc, do you know if it is safe merging the changes despite the formatting error? This is because "undef" is grepped in the test, and it matches with the comments (false positive).

The undef deprecator is a bit unreliable, and ignorable in this case. If you want to, you could file an issue and assign Nuno Lopes. But the real fix is to remove undef entirely and then we can remove the deprecator too. ;-)
